select_when: A post-mortem, retrospective, or performance review is evaluating decisions that were made under genuine uncertainty.
Past decision-makers are being judged for outcomes that were not, in fact, highly predictable given what was known at the time.
Lessons being extracted from a failure are calibrated to the specific failure path as if it were obviously foreseeable, rather than to improving probabilistic reasoning generally.
A rare event is being retrospectively attributed with warning signs that appeared ambiguous or unimportant before the event.
Decision quality is being conflated with outcome quality — good process is being penalized for bad luck or vice versa.
avoid_when: The decision was made with clear, contemporaneous evidence of high failure probability that was ignored — in those cases outcome-based criticism may be warranted.
The review is explicitly anchored to contemporaneous documentation of what was known and uncertain at decision time, not to post-hoc reconstruction.
input_type: post-event evaluation of a decision made under prior uncertainty
output_type: decision quality separation from outcome quality

curation_notes.summary: Hindsight bias curated as a retrospective distortion detection model — activates in post-mortem and review contexts where known outcomes are contaminating evaluation of pre-event decision quality.
open_questions: The model is most powerful when paired with contemporaneous documentation — pre-mortems, timestamped decision memos, explicit probability estimates. The activation surface should be connected to those practices.

select_when.note: Source is explicit about post-mortem distortion, creeping determinism, and outcome-quality/decision-quality conflation as the primary hindsight bias contexts — select_when maps directly to these.

avoid_when.note: Avoid_when conditions are reviewed normalizations — the model should not be used to exculpate genuinely negligent decisions by invoking hindsight bias.

input_type.note: The model activates on retrospective evaluation of decisions — the input is the post-event assessment context, not a prospective decision.

output_type.note: The model's operational output is separating decision quality from outcome quality — the core action that hindsight bias obscures.

reasoning_types.note: Diagnostic — identifying retrospective distortion; probabilistic — recovering the uncertainty distribution that existed at decision time; counterfactual — reconstructing the alternative outcomes that were live; metacognitive — examining the contamination of one's own retrospective assessment.
